devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अन्तर्हिते भगवति सहसैव व्रजाङ्गनाः । अतप्यंस्तमचक्षाणाः करिण्य इव यूथपम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 001 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca antarhite bhagavati sahasaiva vrajāṅganāḥ atapyaṃstamacakṣāṇāḥ kariṇya iva yūthapam || 10 . 30 .

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
When the Lord suddenly disappeared, the damsels from Vraja, like she-elephants which have missed the leader of their herd, were deeply distressed, when they could not see him.

devanagari: गत्यानुरागस्मितविभ्रमेक्षितैर्मनोरमालापविहारविभ्रमैः । आक्षिप्तचित्ताः प्रमदा रमापतेस्तास्ता विचेष्टा जगृहुस्तदात्मिकाः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 0...
english_devnagari: gatyānurāgasmitavibhramekṣitairmanoramālāpavihāravibhramaiḥ ākṣiptacittāḥ pramadā ramāpatestāstā viceṣṭā jagṛhustadātmikāḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: With their hearts captivated by the graceful movements, loving smiles, lustful glances as also by delightful discourses, amorous dalliances and frolics of the Lord of goddess Lakṣmī (Kṛṣṇa), those young damsels mentally identified themselves with him and began to emulate his various acts and sports.

devanagari: गतिस्मितप्रेक्षणभाषणादिषु प्रियाः प्रियस्य प्रतिरूढमूर्तयः । असावहं त्वित्यबलास्तदात्मिका न्यवेदिषुः कृष्णविहारविभ्रमाः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 0...
english_devnagari: gatismitaprekṣaṇabhāṣaṇādiṣu priyāḥ priyasya pratirūḍhamūrtayaḥ asāvahaṃ tvityabalāstadātmikā nyavediṣuḥ kṛṣṇavihāravibhramāḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: Identifying themselves with Lord Kṛṣṇa and exhibiting Kṛṣṇa’s sportful movements and amorous dalliances, the Gopīs, the beloveds of their darling (Kṛṣṇa), whose persons had become (as it were) the duplicates (of Kṛṣṇa) in deportment, graceful smiling, glancing, speech etc., declared (to each other) “I am Kṛṣṇa.”

devanagari: गायन्त्य उच्चैरमुमेव संहता विचिक्युरुन्मत्तकवद्वनाद्वनम् । पप्रच्छुराकाशवदन्तरं बहिर्भूतेषु सन्तं पुरुषं वनस्पतीन् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 004 ॥
english_devnagari: gāyantya uccairamumeva saṃhatā vicikyurunmattakavadvanādvanam papracchurākāśavadantaraṃ bahirbhūteṣu santaṃ puruṣaṃ vanaspatīn || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation: Singing aloud of Kṛṣṇa’s glory in a chorus, they sought for him, like lunatics, from forest to forest, enquiring of trees about that Lord (the Supreme Man) who, like ether, was pervading all the creatures from within and without.

devanagari: दृष्टो वः कच्चिदश्वत्थ प्लक्ष न्यग्रोध नो मनः । नन्दसूनुर्गतो हृत्वा प्रेमहासावलोकनैः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 005 ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭo vaḥ kaccidaśvattha plakṣa nyagrodha no manaḥ nandasūnurgato hṛtvā premahāsāvalokanaiḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: “Oh Aśvattha! Oh Plakṣa! Oh Nyagrodha! (varieties of Indian fig trees). Have you seen the son of Nanda who has absconded, stealing away our hearts by his captivating smiles and bewitching glances?

devanagari: कच्चित्कुरबकाशोक नागपुन्नागचम्पकाः । रामानुजो मानिनीनामितो दर्पहरस्मितः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 006 ॥
english_devnagari: kaccitkurabakāśoka nāgapunnāgacampakāḥ rāmānujo māninīnāmito darpaharasmitaḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: Oh Kurabaka, Asoka, Nāga, Punnāga and Campaka trees! Did the younger brother of Balarāma who humbles the pride of proud women by his smiles pass this way?

devanagari: कच्चित्तुलसि कल्याणि गोविन्दचरणप्रिये । सह त्वालिकुलैर्बिभ्रद्दृष्टस्तेऽतिप्रियोऽच्युतः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 007 ॥
english_devnagari: kaccittulasi kalyāṇi govindacaraṇapriye saha tvālikulairbibhraddṛṣṭaste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: Oh blessed Tulasī (Basil plant) to whom the feet of Lord Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) are so dear! Have you seen Acyuta, so very beloved of you, wearing you (in a wreath) swarming with bees.

devanagari: मालत्यदर्शि वः कच्चिन्मल्लिके जातियूथिके । प्रीतिं वो जनयन् यातः करस्पर्शेन माधवः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 008 ॥
english_devnagari: mālatyadarśi vaḥ kaccinmallike jātiyūthike prītiṃ vo janayan yātaḥ karasparśena mādhavaḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ], [vocative single from √ jan ]

translation: (Addressing various flowering trees) Oh Mālatī! Oh Mallikā! Oh Jāti! Oh Yūthikā! Was Mādhava (Lord of Lakṣmī, Kṛṣṇa) seen by you giving you delight by the touch of his hands, while passing by the way?

devanagari: चूतप्रियालपनसासनकोविदार जम्ब्वर्कबिल्वबकुलाम्रकदम्बनीपाः । येऽन्ये परार्थभवका यमुनोपकूलाः शंसन्तु कृष्णपदवीं रहितात्मनां नः ॥ 10. 30....
english_devnagari: cūtapriyālapanasāsanakovidāra jambvarkabilvabakulāmrakadambanīpāḥ ye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ rah class 10 verb]
[ablative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ rah class 10 verb]

translation: (Invoking other trees) Oh mango tree (of the cūta variety)! Oh Priyāla, Panasa (Jack-fruit tree), Asana, Kovidāra, Jambū (Rose-apple tree), Arka (the sun plant), Bilva (the wood-apple tree), Bakula, Āmra (a variety of mango tree), Kadamba and Nīpa trees and other trees on the bank of the Yamunā—Born as you are for the benefit of others! May you direct the path of Kṛṣṇa to us whose minds are vacant (due to separation from Hari).

devanagari: किं ते कृतं क्षिति तपो बत केशवाङ्घ्रि स्पर्शोत्सवोत्पुलकिताङ्गनहैर्विभासि । अप्यङ्घ्रिसम्भव उरुक्रमविक्रमाद्वा आहो वराहवपुषः परिरम्भण...
english_devnagari: kiṃ te kṛtaṃ kṣiti tapo bata keśavāṅghri sparśotsavotpulakitāṅganahairvibhāsi apyaṅghrisambhava urukramavikramādvā āho varāhavapuṣaḥ parirambhaṇena || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: What austere penance have you performed, Oh Earth, inasmuch as you appear resplendent with your hair standing on their ends through the thrill of joy, at the touch of the feet of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa)! But is this ecstasy due to (the touch of) Kṛṣṇa’s feet just now or is it due to your being bestridden (formerly) by Lord Trivikrama (Viṣṇu, as Vāmana who covered the universe in three steps) or is it caused by the embrace of the Lord when incarnated as a boar (even before Vāmana-incarnation)?

devanagari: अप्येणपत्न्युपगतः प्रिययेह गात्रैस् तन्वन् दृशां सखि सुनिर्वृतिमच्युतो वः । कान्ताङ्गसङ्गकुचकुङ्कुमरञ्जितायाः कुन्दस्रजः कुलपतेरिह वा...
english_devnagari: apyeṇapatnyupagataḥ priyayeha gātrais tanvan dṛśāṃ sakhi sunirvṛtimacyuto vaḥ kāntāṅgasaṅgakucakuṅkumarañjitāyāḥ kundasrajaḥ kulapateriha vāti gandhaḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ tan class 8 verb]
[ablative single from √ rañj ], [genitive single from √ rañj ]

translation: Oh female deer! Here is permeated the fragrance of our leader’s—Lord Kṛṣṇa’s—garland of Kunda flowers tinged with (the scent of) saffron applied to the breast of his lady-love (when he hugged her). Did Kṛṣṇa pass this way along with his beloved, giving delight to your eyes by his graceful limbs Oh friend?

devanagari: बाहुं प्रियांस उपधाय गृहीतपद्मो रामानुजस्तुलसिकालिकुलैर्मदान्धैः । अन्वीयमान इह वस्तरवः प्रणामं किं वाभिनन्दति चरन् प्रणयावलोकैः ॥ 10...
english_devnagari: bāhuṃ priyāṃsa upadhāya gṛhītapadmo rāmānujastulasikālikulairmadāndhaiḥ anvīyamāna iha vastaravaḥ praṇāmaṃ kiṃ vābhinandati caran praṇayāvalokaiḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: (To trees which bent low with the load of fruits) Oh trees! Did the younger brother of Balarāma, placing one (i.e. left) hand on the shoulder of his beloved and holding a lotus in the other (i.e. right) hand (to protect his beloved by warding off) the swarms of bees attracted by (the fragrance of) Tulasī and blinded by the intoxication (caused by the fragrance) which followed him—did Kṛṣṇa (ever) come here and was pleased to accept your low bows with glances full of affection (and appreciation)?

devanagari: पृच्छतेमा लता बाहूनप्याश्लिष्टा वनस्पतेः । नूनं तत्करजस्पृष्टा बिभ्रत्युत्पुलकान्यहो ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 013 ॥
english_devnagari: pṛcchatemā latā bāhūnapyāśliṣṭā vanaspateḥ nūnaṃ tatkarajaspṛṣṭā bibhratyutpulakānyaho || 10 . 30 .

translation: (Oh friends!) Ask these creepers which have embraced the arms (i.e. branches) of their husbands, the trees, as they certainly exhibit the thrill (sprouts or flowers) of joy at the touch of his finger-nails while plucking a flower for his beloved)

devanagari: इत्युन्मत्तवचो गोप्यः कृष्णान्वेषणकातराः । लीला भगवतस्तास्ता ह्यनुचक्रुस्तदात्मिकाः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 014 ॥
english_devnagari: ityunmattavaco gopyaḥ kṛṣṇānveṣaṇakātarāḥ līlā bhagavatastāstā hyanucakrustadātmikāḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: [1] Thus the cowherd women became bewildered in their vain search of Kṛṣṇa and raved in this way. With their hearts engrossed (and as if identical) with him, they began to emulate the divine sports of the Lord.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī’s text does not include io verses from this verse (i.e. vv. 14-23 both inclusive).

devanagari: कस्याचित्पूतनायन्त्याः कृष्णायन्त्यपिबत्स्तनम् । तोकयित्वा रुदत्यन्या पदाहन् शकटायतीम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 015 ॥
english_devnagari: kasyācitpūtanāyantyāḥ kṛṣṇāyantyapibatstanam tokayitvā rudatyanyā padāhan śakaṭāyatīm || 10 . 30 .

translation: One of the Gopīs who imitated Kṛṣṇa sucked the breast of another Gopī who played the part of Pūtanā. Another Gopī emulating Kṛṣṇa, the baby, began to cry and kicked another Gopī who acted the part of a cart (Śakaṭa).

devanagari: दैत्यायित्वा जहारान्यामेको कृष्णार्भभावनाम् । रिङ्गयामास काप्यङ्घ्री कर्षन्ती घोषनिःस्वनैः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 016 ॥
english_devnagari: daityāyitvā jahārānyāmeko kṛṣṇārbhabhāvanām riṅgayāmāsa kāpyaṅghrī karṣantī ghoṣaniḥsvanaiḥ || 10 . 30 .

translation: Acting the role of a demon (viz. Tṛṇāvarta) carried away another Gopī who behaved like an infant Kṛṣṇa, while a third crawled on all fours dragging the feet and thereby making a jingling sound of the anklets.

devanagari: कृष्णरामायिते द्वे तु गोपायन्त्यश्च काश्चन । वत्सायतीं हन्ति चान्या तत्रैका तु बकायतीम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 017 ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇarāmāyite dve tu gopāyantyaśca kāścana vatsāyatīṃ hanti cānyā tatraikā tu bakāyatīm || 10 . 30 .

translation: Two Gopīs enacted[2] the role of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa while others played the part of cowherds. Out of them one struck a third gopī that played the part of Vatsāsura (a demon in the form of a calf), while another (acting as Kṛṣṇa) struck another (fourth) Gopī who enacted the role of the demon Baka.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Historical present is used in some of these verses but for consistent use of tense, past tense is used in translation.

devanagari: आहूय दूरगा यद्वत्कृष्णस्तमनुवर्ततीम् । वेणुं क्वणन्तीं क्रीडन्तीमन्याः शंसन्ति साध्विति ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 018 ॥
english_devnagari: āhūya dūragā yadvatkṛṣṇastamanuvartatīm veṇuṃ kvaṇantīṃ krīḍantīmanyāḥ śaṃsanti sādhviti || 10 . 30 .

[nominative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb]

translation: One imitated Kṛṣṇa shouting out at cows from a distance, while another emulated him and played upon the flute, while others applauded her saying “Well done.”

devanagari: कस्याञ्चित्स्वभुजं न्यस्य चलन्त्याहापरा ननु । कृष्णोऽहं पश्यत गतिं ललितामिति तन्मनाः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 019 ॥
english_devnagari: kasyāñcitsvabhujaṃ nyasya calantyāhāparā nanu kṛṣṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ lal ]

translation: Another Gopī who mentally identified herself with Kṛṣṇa placed her arm on some other Gopī and while following her, told the other “Lo! I am Kṛṣṇa. Look at my graceful gait.”

devanagari: मा भैष्ट वातवर्षाभ्यां तत्त्राणं विहितं मय । इत्युक्त्वैकेन हस्तेन यतन्त्युन्निदधेऽम्बरम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 020 ॥
english_devnagari: mā bhaiṣṭa vātavarṣābhyāṃ tattrāṇaṃ vihitaṃ maya ityuktvaikena hastena yatantyunnidadhe

translation: “Don’t be afraid of tempestuous winds and (driving) shower. Protection against them has been provided by me”. Saying so, another Gopī strained herself and held up (a part of) her garment with one hand (as if it were the mount Govardhana[3]).

devanagari: आरुह्यैका पदाक्रम्य शिरस्याहापरां नृप । दुष्टाहे गच्छ जातोऽहं खलानां ननु दण्डकृत् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 021 ॥
english_devnagari: āruhyaikā padākramya śirasyāhāparāṃ nṛpa duṣṭāhe gaccha jāto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: Treading on the head of another Gopī and mounting thereon (one Gopī) ordered, “Now that I, the wielder of the rod of punishment to the wicked, have incarnated, get out from this place, Oh wicked serpent.”[4]

devanagari: तत्रैकोवाच हे गोपा दावाग्निं पश्यतोल्बणम् । चक्षूंष्याश्वपिदध्वं वो विधास्ये क्षेममञ्जसा ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 022 ॥
english_devnagari: tatraikovāca he gopā dāvāgniṃ paśyatolbaṇam cakṣūṃṣyāśvapidadhvaṃ vo vidhāsye kṣemamañjasā || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: (Imagining that all the Gopas are surrounded by forest conflagration and to vouchsafe their protection) another Gopī said, “Oh cowherds! Lo that raging forest fire. Shut up your eyes quickly. I shall speedily accomplish your welfare (rescue from fire)

devanagari: बद्धान्यया स्रजा काचित्तन्वी तत्र उलूखले । बध्नामि भाण्डभेत्तारं हैयङ्गवमुषं त्विति । भीता सुदृक्पिधायास्यं भेजे भीतिविडम्बनम् ॥ 10. ...
english_devnagari: baddhānyayā srajā kācittanvī tatra ulūkhale badhnāmi bhāṇḍabhettāraṃ haiyaṅgavamuṣaṃ tviti bhītā sudṛkpidhāyāsyaṃ bheje bhītiviḍambanam || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Tied to (a Gopī who represented) a mortar with a wreath by another (Gopī who acted the part of Yaśodā).
23(A).[5] [“Now I bind the thief who has stolen butter and broken a number of (earthen) pots”]. And the lady with beautiful eyes (who was thus bound) covered her face (with hands) and feigned fear (put up a show of trembling with fear).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds this half-verse.

devanagari: एवं कृष्णं पृच्छमाना व्र्ण्दावनलतास्तरून् । व्यचक्षत वनोद्देशे पदानि परमात्मनः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 024 ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ pṛcchamānā vrṇdāvanalatāstarūn vyacakṣata vanoddeśe padāni paramātmanaḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: While they were enquiring of the creepers and trees of Vṛndāvana about Kṛṣṇa, they noticed in a certain part of the forest, the foot-prints of the Supreme soul (Kṛṣṇa).

devanagari: पदानि व्यक्तमेतानि नन्दसूनोर्महात्मनः । लक्ष्यन्ते हि ध्वजाम्भोज वज्राङ्कुशयवादिभिः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 025 ॥
english_devnagari: padāni vyaktametāni nandasūnormahātmanaḥ lakṣyante hi dhvajāmbhoja vajrāṅkuśayavādibhiḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: “It is obvious that the footprints are definitely those of the noble-souled son of Nanda, as they are identified by the marks such as a flag, a lotus, a vajra, a goad and a barley seed”[6]

devanagari: तैस्तैः पदैस्तत्पदवीमन्विच्छन्त्योऽग्रतोऽबलाः । वध्वाः पदैः सुपृक्तानि विलोक्यार्ताः समब्रुवन् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 026 ॥
english_devnagari: taistaiḥ padaistatpadavīmanvicchantyo vadhvāḥ padaiḥ supṛktāni vilokyārtāḥ samabruvan || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: While they went ahead tracing his path by following those footprints, the poor women were sorely distressed at heart to find them interspersed with those of a young woman and spoke to each other.

devanagari: कस्याः पदानि चैतानि याताया नन्दसूनुना । अंसन्यस्तप्रकोष्ठायाः करेणोः करिणा यथा ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 027 ॥
english_devnagari: kasyāḥ padāni caitāni yātāyā nandasūnunā aṃsanyastaprakoṣṭhāyāḥ kareṇoḥ kariṇā yathā || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ yā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: “Whose foot-prints possibly these can be? Who can be this woman who walked in his company with his hand resting on her shoulder, even as a she-elephant with the trunk of her mate on hers.

devanagari: अनयाराधितो नूनं भगवान् हरिरीश्वरः । यन्नो विहाय गोविन्दः प्रीतो यामनयद्रहः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 028 ॥
english_devnagari: anayārādhito nūnaṃ bhagavān harirīśvaraḥ yanno vihāya govindaḥ prīto yāmanayadrahaḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: Certainly the Supreme Ruler, the glorious Lord Hari, has been propitiated[7] by this lady. Hence, being pleased with her, Lord Kṛṣṇa took her to a rendezvous abandoning us all.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
SG.: Here the name of that Gopī is Rādhā is indicated by the text: rādhayati ārādhayati iti śrī-rādhā iti nāmakaraṇaṃ darśiṭam’. Although the text of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa. is silent on this point, all commentators of the Bengal school of Vaiṣṇavism presumed that she was Rādhā and have written interesting extensive commentaries on these verses.

devanagari: धन्या अहो अमी आल्यो गोविन्दाङ्घ्र्यब्जरेणवः । यान् ब्रह्मेशौ रमा देवी दधुर्मूर्ध्न्यघनुत्तये ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 029 ॥
english_devnagari: dhanyā aho amī ālyo govindāṅghryabjareṇavaḥ yān brahmeśau ramā devī dadhurmūrdhnyaghanuttaye || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Oh friends! How blessed are the particles of dust under the Lotus-feet of Lord Govinda—the particles which god Brahmā. Śiva, and goddess Lakṣmī bore on the crowns of their head, for absolving themselves of the sins (which separated them from him. Let us do so, so that by placing the dust on our heads we shall be united with Kṛṣṇa).

devanagari: तस्या अमूनि नः क्षोभं कुर्वन्त्युच्चैः पदानि यत्यैकापहृत्य गोपीनां रहो भुन्क्तेऽच्युताधरम् । न लक्ष्यन्ते पदान्यत्र तस्या नूनं तृणाङ्...
english_devnagari: tasyā amūni naḥ kṣobhaṃ kurvantyuccaiḥ padāni yatyaikāpahṛtya gopīnāṃ raho bhunkte na lakṣyante padānyatra tasyā nūnaṃ tṛṇāṅkuraiḥ khidyatsujātāṅghritalāmunninye preyasīṃ priyaḥ || 10 . 30 .

translation: But these foot-prints of hers cause distress to our hearts inasmuch as only she (out of all Gopīs) has stolen away Kṛṣṇa (who belongs to all of us) and enjoys his kisses in a secret place.

devanagari: इमान्यधिकमग्नानि पदानि वहतो वधूम् । गोप्यः पश्यत कृष्णस्य भाराक्रान्तस्य कामिनः । अत्रावरोपिता कान्ता पुष्पहेतोर्महात्मना ॥ 10. 30. ॥...
english_devnagari: imānyadhikamagnāni padāni vahato vadhūm gopyaḥ paśyata kṛṣṇasya bhārākrāntasya kāminaḥ atrāvaropitā kāntā puṣpahetormahātmanā || 10 . 30 .

translation:
But here her foot-prints are not visible. Most probably, seeing that the tender soles of his beloved’s feet are pricked with sharp and pointed blades of grass, her darling Kṛṣṇa has carried her on his shoulders.
32(A).[8] These foot-prints have been sunk more deeply (in the soil) as he was carrying the young woman. Have a look at these foot-prints of love-lorn Kṛṣṇa (obviously) weighed down under a heavy load.
33(B). (Going a few paces ahead, they observe:) It appears that here he set her down for gathering flowers (for putting them in her braid of hair).
purport:
Footnote [8]:
31(A) Not found in the Text of Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Subodhinī

devanagari: अत्र प्रसूनावचयः प्रियार्थे प्रेयसा कृतः । प्रपदाक्रमण एते पश्यतासकले पदे ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 032 ॥
english_devnagari: atra prasūnāvacayaḥ priyārthe preyasā kṛtaḥ prapadākramaṇa ete paśyatāsakale pade || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: (Proceeding still further) Here flowers are gathered by the Lover for his beloved. Behold these foot-prints which are not entire as he stood on tip-toe.

devanagari: केशप्रसाधनं त्वत्र कामिन्याः कामिना कृतम् । तानि चूडयता कान्तामुपविष्टमिह ध्रुवम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 033 ॥
english_devnagari: keśaprasādhanaṃ tvatra kāminyāḥ kāminā kṛtam tāni cūḍayatā kāntāmupaviṣṭamiha dhruvam || 10 . 30 .

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: (Most probably) on this spot the lust-ful Lord has performed the decoration of the hair of his lady-love. He must have certainly taken his seat here for fastening the flowers in her braid.

devanagari: रेमे तया चात्मरत आत्मारामोऽप्यखण्डितः । कामिनां दर्शयन् दैन्यं स्त्रीणां चैव दुरात्मताम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 034 ॥
english_devnagari: reme tayā cātmarata ātmārāmo kāmināṃ darśayan dainyaṃ strīṇāṃ caiva durātmatām || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: It is with a view to demonstrate to the world the wretched plight of the love-stricken as well as the stone- hearted nature of women that the Lord sported with the lady even though he is ever delighted in his own blissful self, selfsporting Perfect Person unattracted by the amours of women.

devanagari: इत्येवं दर्शयन्त्यस्ताश्चेरुर्गोप्यो विचेतसः । यां गोपीमनयत्कृष्णो विहायान्याः स्त्रियो वने ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 035 ॥
english_devnagari: ityevaṃ darśayantyastāścerurgopyo vicetasaḥ yāṃ gopīmanayatkṛṣṇo vihāyānyāḥ striyo vane || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [locative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ dṛś ], [vocative plural from √ dṛś ]

translation: Showing to each other the traces of Kṛṣṇa’s foot-prints in this way, the Gopīs wandered in a despondent mood (concentrating on Kṛṣṇa). And the Gopī whom Kṛṣṇa (in preference to other Gopīs, and) leaving others in the forest, had brought there, now began to regard herself as the most beautiful of all women. Thought she to herself, “the beloved Lord resorts to me only, abandoning all other Gopīs who too love him.”

devanagari: सा च मेने तदात्मानं वरिष्ठं सर्वयोषिताम् । हित्वा गोपीः कामयाना मामसौ भजते प्रियः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 036 ॥
english_devnagari: sā ca mene tadātmānaṃ variṣṭhaṃ sarvayoṣitām hitvā gopīḥ kāmayānā māmasau bhajate priyaḥ || 10 . 30 .

[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Showing to each other the traces of Kṛṣṇa’s foot-prints in this way, the Gopīs wandered in a despondent mood (concentrating on Kṛṣṇa). And the Gopī whom Kṛṣṇa (in preference to other Gopīs, and) leaving others in the forest, had brought there, now began to regard herself as the most beautiful of all women. Thought she to herself, “the beloved Lord resorts to me only, abandoning all other Gopīs who too love him.”

devanagari: ततो गत्वा वनोद्देशं दृप्ता केशवमब्रवीत् । न पारयेऽहं चलितुं नय मां यत्र ते मनः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 037 ॥
english_devnagari: tato gatvā vanoddeśaṃ dṛptā keśavamabravīt na pāraye
translation: Thereupon, after going to a certain place in the forest, she, being affected with pride, told Śrī Kṛṣṇa, “I am not able to walk any further. Therefore (give me a lift and) take me whither-so-ever you please”.

devanagari: एवमुक्तः प्रियामाह स्कन्ध आरुह्यतामिति । ततश्चान्तर्दधे कृष्णः सा वधूरन्वतप्यत ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 038 ॥
english_devnagari: evamuktaḥ priyāmāha skandha āruhyatāmiti tataścāntardadhe kṛṣṇaḥ sā vadhūranvatapyata || 10 . 30 .

[imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: When requested thus, he told his beloved, “Mount on my shoulder” and he disappeared. And the lady lamented in repentance.

devanagari: हा नाथ रमण प्रेष्ठ क्वासि क्वासि महाभुज । दास्यास्ते कृपणाया मे सखे दर्शय सन्निधिम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 039 ॥
english_devnagari: hā nātha ramaṇa preṣṭha kvāsi kvāsi mahābhuja dāsyāste kṛpaṇāyā me sakhe darśaya sannidhim || 10 . 30 .

[nominative plural from √ das ], [vocative plural from √ das ], [accusative plural from √ das ]

translation: “Oh Lord! My darling! Oh most beloved one! Where have you gone? I am your miserable slave. Be pleased with me to reveal your Presence, Oh mighty armed Lord!”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अन्विच्छन्त्यो भगवतो मार्गं गोप्योऽविदूरितः । ददृशुः प्रियविश्लेषान्मोहितां दुःखितां सखीम् ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 040 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca anvicchantyo bhagavato mārgaṃ gopyo dadṛśuḥ priyaviśleṣānmohitāṃ duḥkhitāṃ sakhīm || 10 . 30 .

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: While the (other) cowherd women went on tracing the path of the Lord, they noticed from a distance, their distressed friend bewildered through her separation from her darling.

devanagari: तया कथितमाकर्ण्य मानप्राप्तिं च माधवात् । अवमानं च दौरात्म्याद्विस्मयं परमं ययुः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 041 ॥
english_devnagari: tayā kathitamākarṇya mānaprāptiṃ ca mādhavāt avamānaṃ ca daurātmyādvismayaṃ paramaṃ yayuḥ || 10 . 30 .

[vocative single from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ u class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ u class 1 verb]

translation: Listening to her report as to how she was received with honour by Madhava (Kṛṣṇa) and was humiliated by him through her own wickedness, they were extremely astonished.

devanagari: ततोऽविशन् वनं चन्द्र ज्योत्स्ना यावद्विभाव्यते । तमः प्रविष्टमालक्ष्य ततो निववृतुः स्त्रियः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 042 ॥
english_devnagari: tato tamaḥ praviṣṭamālakṣya tato nivavṛtuḥ striyaḥ || 10 . 30 .
translation: They further probed into the forest (for Kṛṣṇa) so long as the moon-light was available. Seeing that darkness was setting in, they returned from that spot.

devanagari: तन्मनस्कास्तदलापास्तद्विचेष्टास्तदात्मिकाः । तद्गुणानेव गायन्त्यो नात्मगाराणि सस्मरुः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 043 ॥
english_devnagari: tanmanaskāstadalāpāstadviceṣṭāstadātmikāḥ tadguṇāneva gāyantyo nātmagārāṇi sasmaruḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: With their minds absorbed in him, they talked about Kṛṣṇa only; they imitated his movements and sang about his excellences. They became oblivious of their persons, as well as of their homes.

devanagari: पुनः पुलिनमागत्य कालिन्द्याः कृष्णभावनाः । समवेता जगुः कृष्णं तदागमनकाङ्क्षिताः ॥ 10. 30. ॥ 044 ॥
english_devnagari: punaḥ pulinamāgatya kālindyāḥ kṛṣṇabhāvanāḥ samavetā jaguḥ kṛṣṇaṃ tadāgamanakāṅkṣitāḥ || 10 . 30 .

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kāṅkṣ class 1 verb]

translation:
Returning to the sandy bank of the Yamunā, the Gopīs contemplated on Kṛṣṇa only. Anxiously waiting for his arrival, they sang of Kṛṣṇa in a chorus.
